incorrects.description: Student placed wrong number of symbols on first attempt

steps.text: Not quite. Count by 5s until you reach 35. How many times do you count?

incorrects.description: Student placed wrong number of symbols on second attempt

params.description: Liam's value 35 highlights in data table.

steps.text: Look at Liam's value: 35 books. The key shows each symbol equals 5 books. Count by 5s: 5, 10, 15, 20, 25, 30, 35. How many 5s is that?

incorrects.description: Student placed wrong number of symbols three or more times. System models the answer.

params.description: 7 symbols appear one by one in Liam row, skip-counting by 5s as each appears: 5, 10, 15, 20, 25, 30, 35.

steps.text: Let me show you. Liam read 35 books. Each symbol stands for 5 books. I count by 5s: 5, 10, 15, 20, 25, 30, 35. That's 7 times I counted 5. So I place 7 symbols. The answer is 7 symbols because 35 equals seven groups of 5.
